description: Contract + behavioral-track invariant/property ledger. Closes AR-M2 from specs/reviews/arc-progress-codex.md. Scope: specs/contracts/*.md + specs/behavioral/*.md. Out of scope: CLAUDE.md hard invariants, specs/evidence.md invariants, ADR-level invariants (documented exclusion per Slice 25 Codex challenger MED #9 fold-in; deferred to v0.2).

enforcement_state_semantics.test-enforced: Invariant id appears as a token in at least one binding_ref test-file path (describe/it title or assertion message). Eligibility: required default for any contract invariant stated as 'MUST reject', 'MUST enforce', or 'is rejected'.
enforcement_state_semantics.audit-only: Invariant id appears as a token in scripts/audit.mjs. Eligibility: audit-layer or discipline invariants (session hygiene, lane discipline, citation rule).
enforcement_state_semantics.static-anchor: Invariant enforced structurally by schema export or superRefine; binding_refs name the schema file plus at least one test file that parses that schema. Eligibility: structural invariants whose id may not appear as a test title but whose enforcement cannot regress without the schema change firing a named test failure. Grandfathered scope_ids entries in tests/contracts/cross-model-challenger.test.ts count as binding_refs for step.md and workflow.md.
enforcement_state_semantics.prose-only: Policy statement not directly testable (e.g. when the challenger should be invoked; what the challenger cannot replace). Eligibility: behavioral-track meta-invariants only. Requires `rationale` field. A contract 'MUST reject' invariant cannot be prose-only.
enforcement_state_semantics.phase2-property: Reserved for Phase 2 property-test harness; not enforced at Phase 1 verify time. Requires `target_slice` (29 = property registry scaffold) + `reopen_condition`.

out_of_scope_notes: AR-H1 persisted-shape binding for invariants to artifacts is deferred to Slice 26a. Optional `artifact_ids` ledger field may be added in a later slice; absence here is intentional and not a silent omission.

CLAUDE.md §Hard invariants 1-10, specs/evidence.md Invariant 1-N, and ADR-0001/0002/0003 reopen/authority constraints are out of scope for Slice 25 per Codex challenger MED #9 fold-in disposition; expansion is v0.2 scope.

invariants.rationale: Kind-variant binding enforced by Step discriminated union in src/schemas/step.ts. The scope_ids literal in cross-model-challenger.test.ts (grandfathered-allowlist, Slice 24 MED #5) binds the id — dropping STEP-I1 from step.md fires a named test failure. Upgrading to test_title binding is Slice 27 scope (workflow/step v0.2).
Non-empty routes enforced structurally on Step.routes. Scope_ids binding ensures id cannot disappear silently. Upgrade deferred to Slice 27.
Budget bounds enforced structurally on Step.budgets. Scope_ids binding ensures id cannot disappear silently. Upgrade deferred to Slice 27.
Protocol required enforced structurally on Step.protocol. Scope_ids binding ensures id cannot disappear silently. Upgrade deferred to Slice 27.
Unique step ids enforced by Workflow superRefine in src/schemas/workflow.ts. Slice 27 workflow.md v0.2 exits the skeleton grandfather and binds WF-I1 to a per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed entry_modes.start_at references enforced by Workflow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed phase step references enforced by Workflow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Closed route targets enforced by Workflow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade from static-anchor to test-enforced via per-invariant titled negative test.
Unique entry-mode names enforced by Workflow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test.
Unique phase ids enforced by Workflow superRefine. Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test.

schema_version=2 enforced by the Workflow literal field on WorkflowBody (before superRefine). Slice 27 upgrade adds per-invariant titled negative test rejecting non-'2' schema_version values (Codex challenger LOW #8 fold-in — prior phrasing said 'surplus unknown literals' which conflated literal-mismatch with strict-key rejection).

Terminal reachability enforced by Workflow superRefine via iterative fixpoint over terminal-reaching steps. New in Slice 27 workflow.md v0.2 — structural requirement for dogfood-run-0: a workflow fixture where some step cannot reach @complete/@stop/@escalate/@handoff would bootstrap a Run that could never emit run.closed.

No dead steps enforced by Workflow superRefine via BFS from every entry_mode.start_at. New in Slice 27 workflow.md v0.2 — a declared step not reachable from any entry is a silent declaration bug for dogfood fixtures. The reachability pass is gated on WF-I1/WF-I4/WF-I2 structural soundness (allEntryStartsKnown precondition) so a single WF-I2 violation does not cascade into WF-I9 noise (Codex challenger MED #4 fold-in).

Pass-route presence enforced by Workflow superRefine — every step's routes map must contain key 'pass'. GateEvaluatedEvent.outcome is z.enum(['pass','fail']) uniformly across all three gate kinds (src/schemas/event.ts), so a successful gate outcome routes through routes['pass']. New in Slice 27 workflow.md v0.2 as Codex challenger HIGH #1 fold-in — closes the WF-I8 graph-reachable-but-runtime-unselectable hole (fixture routes like { success: '@complete' } would satisfy WF-I8 and stall at runtime). `fail`-route presence deferred to v0.3 / Phase 2.

Policy statement about when the challenger is invoked (ratchet-changing surfaces). Judgment call by operator; no machine-enforceable surface. Permitted prose-only per behavioral-track eligibility rule.

Policy statement about what the challenger MAY NOT replace (authority mapping / reference evidence / fixture parity / differential tests / state-machine tests / migration rehearsal). Enforces through methodology discipline, not a schema surface. Permitted prose-only per behavioral-track eligibility rule.

Policy statement about compiler direction authorship (prose → YAML is hand-authored; YAML → prose is compiler-owned). The catalog compiler has not landed at v0.1; no round-trip surface exists yet. The SENTINEL test in tests/contracts/prose-yaml-parity.test.ts fires when compiler markers appear, at which point PROSE-YAML-I3 is re-classified. Permitted prose-only with target-promotion trigger.
